name: Brazilian Black Beans & Rice ( Original )
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon vinegar (reserve to add at the end)
1 pound bulk sausage (e.g., boneless pork ribs or rolled roasts)
1 pound stew meat (beef chuck or leftover roast)
1/2 pound smoked link sausage, cut into bite-sized pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the beans and soak them overnight in a large bowl with enough water to cover them well.
2. In a skillet, brown the bulk sausage until no longer pink. Drain excess fat.
3. Add the remaining meats (stew meat, ham, and smoked sausage) to the pot and brown them.
4. Dice and sauté bell pepper, onions, garlic, celery, parsley, oregano, basil, bay leaves, and clove with a pinch of salt and pepper until fragrant.
5. Add beans and enough water to cover all ingredients by at least two inches. Stir in bouillon cubes and bring the mixture to a boil (if using a pot).
6. Reduce heat to low and simmer for three hours on high or six hours on low in a crockpot.
7. If cooking on a stovetop, continue simmering for six hours, stirring every hour or two to prevent sticking.
8. About an hour before serving, stir in vinegar.
9. Serve the beans and meat over rice (brown rice is recommended but white rice works too).
10. Garnish with fresh chopped onion and serve with a mixed green salad on the side.
